acts.summary: Establishes Travel Alberta as a provincial corporation mandated to market Alberta's tourism assets, attractions, and opportunities in domestic, national, and international markets, and to promote and support development and growth of the visitor economy.

sections.title: Establishment of Travel Alberta
sections.description: Establishes Travel Alberta as a corporation with the mandate to promote Alberta as a tourism destination, fund destination marketing, operate visitor information services, and support the growth of the visitor economy.

services.name: Access Travel Alberta's destination marketing and visitor information
services.ms_name: Plan travel and get help with travel problems

services.name: Develop and implement Alberta's tourism strategy

sections.title: Objects and powers of Travel Alberta
sections.description: Travel Alberta may carry out programs to promote tourism, enter into agreements, provide grants and financial assistance, conduct research, and do anything necessary to fulfill its mandate.

services.name: Invest in or develop a tourism product or experience
services.ms_name: Grow tourism investment and destinations

sections.title: Traveller protection provisions
sections.description: Provisions related to the protection of travellers using tourism services in Alberta, including oversight of travel agents and tour operators.

services.name: Check if a travel agent or tour operator is registered
services.ms_name: Plan travel and get help with travel problems

services.name: Report a travel-related complaint
services.ms_name: Plan travel and get help with travel problems

acts.summary: Imposes a tourism levy on the purchase price of temporary accommodation in Alberta. Requires operators and online brokers to register, collect the levy from purchasers, and remit it to Alberta Tax and Revenue Administration. The rate increased to 6% effective April 2026.

sections.title: Levy on accommodation
sections.description: Every purchaser of accommodation shall pay a levy equal to 6% of the purchase price. The levy applies to hotels, motels, lodges, B&Bs, short-term rentals, and similar temporary accommodation.

services.name: File and remit the tourism levy
services.ms_name: Collect and administer the tourism levy

sections.title: Registration of operators
sections.description: Every operator must register with the Minister before selling or providing accommodation. Online brokers collecting payment on behalf of operators must also register.

services.name: Register as a tourism levy collector
services.ms_name: Collect and administer the tourism levy

sections.title: Collection and remittance
sections.description: Operators must collect the tourism levy from purchasers and remit it to the Minister in accordance with the regulations. Online brokers collecting payment must also collect and remit the levy.

services.name: Register as a tourism levy collector
services.ms_name: Collect and administer the tourism levy

services.name: File and remit the tourism levy
services.ms_name: Collect and administer the tourism levy

acts.summary: Creates a framework for developing all-season commercial tourism resorts on designated Crown lands in Alberta. Establishes a sole lifecycle regulator, a new land classification (All-Season Resort Areas), and processes for development approvals balancing economic growth, environmental protection, and Indigenous community opportunities.

sections.title: Designation of All-Season Resort Areas
sections.description: The Lieutenant Governor in Council may designate Crown land as an All-Season Resort Area suitable for four-season commercial tourism development.

services.name: Get information about all-season resort areas
services.ms_name: Grow tourism investment and destinations

sections.title: All-Season Resorts Policy
sections.description: The Minister must establish and maintain an All-Season Resorts Policy setting out the vision, principles, and goals for resort development, including environmental requirements, Indigenous consultation, and public interest considerations.

services.name: Get information about all-season resort areas
services.ms_name: Grow tourism investment and destinations

sections.title: Development approvals
sections.description: The regulator reviews development proposals, conducts impact assessments, and may approve, modify, or reject proposals for all-season resort developments on designated Crown lands.

services.name: Propose an all-season resort development
services.ms_name: Grow tourism investment and destinations

acts.full_name: Fairness and Safety in Sport Act
acts.summary: Establishes requirements for fairness and safety in sport participation in Alberta. Requires Provincial Sport Organizations, post-secondary institutions, and school authorities to develop and maintain policies for sport participation categories, athlete protections, and the creation of mixed-sex leagues and divisions.

sections.title: Application
sections.description: The Act applies to Provincial Sport Organizations, public post-secondary institutions, independent academic institutions, and school authorities with students of appropriate age.

services.name: Comply with fairness and safety in sport requirements (organizations)
services.ms_name: Ensure fairness and safety in sport

sections.title: Policies
sections.description: Boards must establish, implement, and maintain fairness and safety policies for each relevant sport, including rules for participation categories and support for mixed-sex divisions where numbers warrant.

services.name: Get information about the Fairness and Safety in Sport Act
services.ms_name: Ensure fairness and safety in sport

services.name: Comply with fairness and safety in sport requirements (organizations)
services.ms_name: Ensure fairness and safety in sport

sections.title: Reporting requirements
sections.description: Boards must provide information to the Minister on complaints received and requests related to sport participation, ensuring transparency and accountability in the sport system.

services.name: Comply with fairness and safety in sport requirements (organizations)
services.ms_name: Ensure fairness and safety in sport

sections.title: Ministerial guidelines
sections.description: The Minister may establish guidelines to assist boards in developing and implementing fairness and safety policies.

services.name: Get information about the Fairness and Safety in Sport Act
services.ms_name: Ensure fairness and safety in sport

acts.summary: Continues the Alberta Sport, Recreation, Parks and Wildlife Foundation as Alberta Sport Connection, a Crown corporation that advises the Minister on sport policy, distributes funding to provincial sport organizations, and coordinates the provincial sport system including grant programs, awards, and participation initiatives.

sections.title: Establishment of Alberta Sport Connection
sections.description: Continues the Foundation as Alberta Sport Connection, a corporation of up to 10 members appointed by the Lieutenant Governor in Council to advise the Minister on sport and recreation policy.

services.name: Coordinate provincial sport policy and funding

sections.title: Objects and powers
sections.description: Alberta Sport Connection may provide grants, awards, and financial assistance to sport, physical activity, and recreation organizations, conduct research, and carry out programs to promote active participation.

services.name: Apply for a sport, physical activity, or recreation operating grant
services.ms_name: Fund sport, physical activity and recreation organizations

services.name: Apply for a sport and recreation participation grant
services.ms_name: Fund sport, physical activity and recreation organizations

services.name: Apply for a major sport events grant
services.ms_name: Fund sport, physical activity and recreation organizations

services.name: Nominate someone for a sport or recreation award
services.ms_name: Recognize contributions to sport and recreation
